summary: Small two-storey rectangular building with continuous horizontal window bands on both floors, light-colored corner and band trim, sandstone-like middle wall courses, and a stepped brick gable roof. A low white slab border surrounds the ground perimeter on a shallow foundation trench.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with a centered stepped gable roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long center axis (front/back mirror)

elements: continuous horizontal windows (narrow strip windows) on each floor on long sides
stepped brick gable roof with small stone slab cap at peak and step corners
white slab border/low wall around building base (foundation edging)
horizontal white trim band between floors and at top corners
rebuild_hints: Footprint is a rectangle with long sides containing two horizontal rows of narrow windows each spanning most of the length; count approximately 9–11 window panes per long side.
Walls: use sandstone-like blocks for main mid sections, framed by full-height quartz (white) corner columns and a continuous quartz band between floors and at top for a boxy trim.
Roof: construct a centered gable made of brick blocks stepped upward in 1-block terraces toward the ridge; place stone/slab caps at the short-end ridge steps.
Perimeter: place a 1-block-high continuous white slab/edge around the base, set flush with ground and extending one block outward.
Height: each floor has ~3 blocks of wall height (windows centered in those courses); allow 2–3 blocks of stepped roof height above the top trim.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone and quartz are inferred visually; they may be sandstone/sandstone_smooth and quartz_block variants.
Dimensions: width/depth/height are estimated from images and top views; actual block counts may differ by ±2–3 blocks.
Interior layout and presence of doors or internal floors/stairs are not visible and thus unknown.
Foundation depth and whether the white perimeter is full blocks or slabs is uncertain (appears as slabs but could be blocks).
